reawakendBeginnerGuide.title: Reawakened Beginner Guide
reawakendBeginnerGuide.intro: Start your journey in Reawakened by mastering the hunter loop: enter gates, collect loot, upgrade your class, and climb the ranks. Every system builds on the previous one, so understanding the basics early makes everything else smoother.

steps.title: Choose Your Class
steps.description: Your class defines your combat role. Mage deals fast magic damage, Warrior absorbs hits as a front-liner, Assassin bursts targets at close range, Archer attacks safely from a distance, and Healer supports teammates. Beginners should start with Warrior or Mage.

steps.title: Enter Your First Gate
steps.description: Gates are timed dungeons with wave-based combat and a final boss. Start on F-rank gates to learn the mechanics. Clear all waves before the timer runs out and defeat the gate boss to earn gear and XP.

steps.title: Reroll Your Traits
steps.description: Traits are passive modifiers that stack with your class. After your first gate run, spend your gold rerolling traits that do not match your class. Offense traits benefit Mage and Assassin, defense traits benefit Warrior, and utility traits help all classes.

steps.title: Pick a Progression Path
steps.description: Paths shape your long-term growth. Choose Combat Path for faster gate clears, Defense Path for higher survivability, or Balance Path if you are still exploring. Stick to one path through your first 20 levels.

steps.title: Farm Gates for Gear
steps.description: Repeat gates at your current rank tier until your gear feels strong. Each gate difficulty has its own loot pool. Move to the next rank tier only when your gear can handle the jump in enemy strength.

steps.title: Complete Your Rank-Up Challenge
steps.description: When you reach the level cap for your current hunter rank, a rank-up challenge unlocks. This is a harder gate with stricter conditions. Completing it promotes you to the next rank tier and unlocks new gate difficulties.

quickTips: Join the official Discord at discord.gg/reawakened for live code alerts and community guides.
Redeem all active codes before your first gate run to get an XP and stat head start.
Traits compound over many gate runs. Rerolling early saves time in the long run.
Group runs clear gates faster and split the timer pressure. Use the in-game party system for B-rank and above.

reawakendCodesGuide.title: Reawakened Codes
reawakendCodesGuide.intro: Reawakened codes give free XP boosts, stat buffs, and cosmetic items. Codes are released during updates, milestones, and special events. Redeem them quickly because most codes have a limited number of uses.

cards.name: Active Code Rewards
cards.description: Current codes give XP multipliers, stat bonuses, and cosmetic unlocks. Check our Codes page for the full up-to-date list updated after every drop.

cards.name: How to Redeem a Code
cards.description: Open the in-game menu, tap the Codes button, type or paste the code exactly as shown, then press Confirm. Codes are case-sensitive.

cards.name: Code Expiry and Limits
cards.description: Most Reawakened codes expire after a set number of total redemptions or a time window. Redeem immediately when a new code drops to guarantee your reward.

cards.name: Where Codes Are Announced
cards.description: New codes are posted first in the official Reawakened Discord server. Follow the announcements channel to get alerts the moment new codes go live.

milestones: Stack code XP bonuses before a gate run
Join Discord for instant code alerts

reawakendClassesGuide.title: Reawakened Classes Guide
reawakendClassesGuide.intro: Reawakened has five playable classes, each with a distinct combat role, stat focus, and gate playstyle. Your class choice is one of the most important decisions at the start of every run.

items.name: Mage
items.description: High spell damage with fast cast speed. Mage clears gates quickly but has lower HP. Best for players who want maximum gate clear speed and burst damage from a safe distance.

items.name: Warrior
items.description: High base health and strong physical damage. Warrior is the easiest class for beginners because it can absorb hits from multiple enemies without losing gate runs easily.

items.name: Assassin
items.description: Explosive critical hit damage at close range. Assassin is a high-risk, high-reward class that rewards players who can dodge well and hit fast before enemies can respond.

items.name: Archer
items.description: Consistent damage output from long range with good mobility. Archer suits players who want a balance of safety and steady damage without committing to melee range.

items.name: Healer
items.description: Team healing, shields, and buff application. Healer is best in group gate runs where sustaining teammates unlocks harder difficulties faster than any DPS class could solo.

items.name: Dual Blade
items.description: Very high attack speed with moderate individual hit damage. Dual Blade rewards aggressive players who want to stack attack speed traits and overwhelm enemies with volume of hits.

items.name: Tank
items.description: Maximum HP and damage reduction with crowd control abilities. Tank is the top choice for defense path builds and anchoring group runs on the highest difficulty gates.

items.name: Shadow Monarch
items.description: A special late-game class inspired by Solo Leveling. Unlock requirements and exact mechanics are tied to high hunter rank progression milestones in the current version.

reawakendTraitsGuide.title: Reawakened Traits Guide
reawakendTraitsGuide.intro: Traits are passive modifiers that stack with your class abilities and gear. Building the right trait set for your class dramatically increases gate clear speed and survivability.

solutions.name: Offense Traits
solutions.description: Increase attack power, magic damage, critical hit rate, and critical damage. Recommended for Mage, Assassin, and Archer builds focused on fast gate clearing.

solutions.name: Defense Traits
solutions.description: Add health, damage reduction, and regeneration bonuses. Ideal for Warrior and Tank builds that need to sustain through heavy enemy pressure on higher rank gates.

solutions.name: Utility Traits
solutions.description: Reduce ability cooldowns, boost movement speed, and increase XP and gold gain per gate run. Utility traits are a strong secondary investment for all classes.

solutions.name: Rare and Legendary Traits
solutions.description: Powerful traits with major stat multipliers or unique effects. They appear at low probability during high-tier rerolls and are the target for optimized late-game builds.

managementTips: Match your first two trait slots to your class role before experimenting with utility.
Reroll early and often. Spending gold on traits early beats saving for gear upgrades.
Once your build direction is clear, prioritize locking in traits that scale with your chosen path.
Check community builds in the Discord for proven trait combinations per class.

reawakendPathsGuide.title: Reawakened Progression Paths
reawakendPathsGuide.intro: Paths in Reawakened shape your long-term stat growth and build direction. Choosing the right path for your class early makes every gate run more efficient and every rank-up challenge more achievable.

cards.name: Combat Path
cards.description: Focuses on raw attack power and gate clear speed. Combat Path benefits Mage, Assassin, Archer, and Dual Blade the most. It is the fastest path for solo gate farming at all rank tiers.

cards.name: Defense Path
cards.description: Builds toward maximum HP scaling and damage reduction. Defense Path turns Warrior and Tank into near-unkillable gate runners for higher difficulty content where survivability is the primary barrier.

cards.name: Support Path
cards.description: Amplifies healing output and team-wide buff strength. Support Path is the correct choice for Healer in group runs. It makes your presence directly responsible for the team reaching harder gate tiers.

cards.name: Balance Path
cards.description: Spreads growth across offense, defense, and utility evenly. Balance Path is a good early choice if you are still testing different classes or have not committed to a specific build direction yet.

highlights: Faster gate clear speed
Near-immortal survivability
Team-wide damage boost
Flexible early progression

reawakendGatesGuide.title: Reawakened Gates Guide
reawakendGatesGuide.intro: Gates are the core gameplay loop in Reawakened. Each gate rank introduces new enemy patterns, stronger bosses, and better loot. Understanding what to expect at each rank tier helps you progress without wasting gate attempts.

regions.name: F-Rank Gate
regions.description: Entry-level gate with simple wave mechanics and a straightforward boss. Best for hunters at rank 0 to 10 learning the core loop. Drop pool includes basic gear and early XP boosts.

regions.name: E-Rank Gate
regions.description: Slightly faster enemy spawns and the first appearance of elite enemies between waves. Recommended for rank 10 to 20. Drops better gear and more XP than F-rank gates.

regions.name: D-Rank Gate
regions.description: Introduces mechanics like timed elites and multi-phase bosses. Recommended for rank 20 to 35. The loot pool here includes mid-tier gear with trait compatibility bonuses.

regions.name: C-Rank Gate
regions.description: Multiple elite waves with a boss that has two or more phases. Recommended for rank 35 to 50. C-rank is the most efficient XP and gear source before entering endgame content.

regions.name: B-Rank Gate
regions.description: Complex enemy patterns and high HP enemies. B-rank gates are best cleared in groups of two or more. Drops rare gear with powerful passive effects not available in lower-rank gates.

regions.name: A-Rank Gate
regions.description: Endgame content with the highest stat requirements in the current version. A-rank drops include the best gear and the rarest trait-compatible equipment currently in the game.

reawakendBossesGuide.title: Reawakened Bosses Guide
reawakendBossesGuide.intro: Every gate in Reawakened ends with a boss encounter. Understanding each boss type helps you prepare the right build, avoid one-shot mechanics, and farm efficiently for the drops you need.

creatures.name: Gate Guardian
creatures.description: The final boss of every gate. Each rank tier has a different guardian variant with increasing HP, damage output, and phase complexity. Defeating the guardian is required to complete the gate and claim your loot.

creatures.name: Shadow Monarch Spawn
creatures.description: A powerful mid-gate elite enemy that mimics the Shadow Monarch aesthetic from the game theme. Has a one-shot ability with a visible wind-up. Dodge it or use a defensive cooldown.

creatures.name: Cursed Spirit
creatures.description: A fast-moving enemy that applies a damage-over-time debuff on hit. Prioritize Cursed Spirits quickly to prevent debuff stacking, especially on lower-health classes like Assassin and Mage.

creatures.name: Dungeon Golem
creatures.description: A heavily armored gate enemy with damage reduction and slow but heavy attacks. Golems appear in D-rank gates and above. Use abilities with armor-piercing effects for faster clears.

creatures.name: Undead Warrior
creatures.description: A common gate enemy with above-average HP and predictable melee attacks. Undead Warriors are straightforward but appear in large groups in higher rank gates, creating pressure through volume.

creatures.name: Ranked Challenge Boss
creatures.description: A special boss encountered during rank-up challenges only. Has more complex mechanics than standard gate bosses and requires a build specifically tuned for the challenge tier you are attempting.

creatures.name: Gate Boss Phase 2
creatures.description: Some bosses in C-rank and above enter a second phase after dropping below 50% HP. Phase 2 includes new attack patterns and often a temporary stat increase. Stay mobile during the transition.

reawakendGearGuide.title: Reawakened Gear Guide
reawakendGearGuide.intro: Gear in Reawakened scales with gate rank and hunter tier. Upgrading your equipment at the right stages of progression keeps your gate clears efficient and prevents you from hitting survivability walls.

items.name: F-Rank Gear
items.description: Entry-level equipment from F-rank gates and starter codes. Provides modest stat bonuses. Replace these as soon as E-rank drops become available.

items.name: E-Rank Gear
items.description: Improved base stats from E and D-rank gate drops. Suitable for hunters in the rank 10 to 25 range. The jump from F to E gear is the most noticeable early power spike.

items.name: D-Rank Gear
items.description: Mid-tier equipment with additional trait slots that let you apply trait bonuses directly to your gear. Farmed from D and C-rank gates in the rank 20 to 40 range.

items.name: C-Rank Gear
items.description: High-stat gear from C-rank gate drops with better trait compatibility and set bonuses. This is the primary gear tier for hunters pushing into the B-rank gate difficulty.

items.name: B-Rank Gear
items.description: Endgame equipment for advanced hunters. B-rank gear has powerful passive effects and high base stats that significantly increase your survivability and damage output on expert content.

items.name: Cosmetic Gear
items.description: Visual-only items available from codes and special events. Cosmetics have no effect on stats or gate performance but are a popular reward type in the Roblox economy.

reawakendLevelingGuide.title: Reawakened Leveling Guide
reawakendLevelingGuide.intro: Efficient leveling in Reawakened means knowing which gates to farm at each rank tier and using XP boost codes at the right time. Pushing into gates that are too hard too early wastes time and resources.

sections.name: Rank 0 to 15: F-Gate Loop
sections.description: Run F-rank gates repeatedly until your gear is fully upgraded from F-rank drops. Use any active XP boost codes before this phase. This stage should feel smooth and fast.

sections.name: Rank 15 to 30: E and D Gates
sections.description: Move to E-rank gates when your gear is upgraded. Transition to D-rank gates when E-rank drops stop being upgrades. The first rank-up challenge lands somewhere in this range.

sections.name: Rank 30 to 45: C-Gate Farming
sections.description: C-rank gates are the most efficient XP and gear source for the mid-game. Group runs here significantly cut clear times. Target C-rank until your gear is fully upgraded for this tier.

sections.name: Rank 45+: B and A Gate Push
sections.description: B-rank and above is endgame content. Join a group for B-rank clears and invest in your best available build before attempting A-rank. These gates have the rarest drops and highest XP payouts.

reawakendBestBuilds.title: Reawakened Best Builds
reawakendBestBuilds.intro: The strongest Reawakened builds combine the right class, trait set, path, and gear for a specific purpose. Each build below is optimized for its intended gate role and tested across multiple rank tiers.

priorities.name: Mage DPS Build
priorities.description: Mage class with Combat Path and stacked magic damage offense traits. Best gate clear speed in the current version for solo farming. Use C-rank gear with trait compatibility bonuses for maximum output.

priorities.name: Warrior Tank Build
priorities.description: Warrior class with Defense Path and full defense trait investment. Near-unkillable in B-rank gates. Ideal for sustained farming without losing gate runs on difficult content.

priorities.name: Assassin Burst Build
priorities.description: Assassin class with Combat Path and critical hit offense traits. Extremely high burst damage per second but requires good dodge timing to compensate for low HP. High ceiling, high risk.

priorities.name: Archer Sustain Build
priorities.description: Archer class with Balance Path and a mix of offense and utility traits. Safe and consistent damage from range. Best for players who want steady performance without high skill requirements.

priorities.name: Healer Group Build
priorities.description: Healer class with Support Path and utility traits focused on heal strength and buff duration. Unlocks harder gate difficulties in group runs by keeping the party alive through boss phase two transitions.

priorities.name: Tank Gate Build
priorities.description: Tank class with Defense Path and full health scaling traits. Maximum durability with crowd control. Best used as a group anchor for A-rank attempts where boss damage can one-shot lighter classes.

reawakendHunterRanks.title: Reawakened Hunter Ranks
reawakendHunterRanks.intro: Hunter ranks in Reawakened mark your progression through the game. Each tier unlocks new gate difficulties, better gear pools, and higher-stakes rank-up challenges that test your build.

achievements.title: First Gate Clear
achievements.description: Complete your first F-rank gate and claim your starter loot.

achievements.title: First Code Redeemed
achievements.description: Redeem your first active code for a free XP and stat boost.

achievements.title: Trait Unlock
achievements.description: Unlock your first trait slot and apply an offense or defense trait.

achievements.title: E-Rank Gate Clear
achievements.description: Complete an E-rank gate solo and collect your first E-rank gear drop.

achievements.title: First Rank-Up Challenge
achievements.description: Attempt and complete your first rank-up challenge to promote to the next hunter tier.

achievements.title: Second Trait Slot
achievements.description: Unlock and fill your second trait slot with a matching modifier for your class.

achievements.title: D-Rank Gate Clear
achievements.description: Clear a D-rank gate and upgrade your gear to the D-rank tier.

achievements.title: Path Committed
achievements.description: Choose and invest fully into a Combat, Defense, or Support progression path.

achievements.title: C-Rank Gate Attempted
achievements.description: Enter and clear a C-rank gate with your current build.

achievements.title: B-Rank Gate Clear
achievements.description: Complete a B-rank gate, alone or in a group, and claim endgame loot.

achievements.title: Full Build Complete
achievements.description: Finalize your class, path, gear, and trait combination for endgame content.

achievements.title: A-Rank Unlocked
achievements.description: Reach the hunter rank required to attempt A-rank gate content.

reawakendGameFAQ.title: Reawakened FAQ
reawakendGameFAQ.intro: Common questions about Reawakened gameplay, codes, classes, and progression answered concisely.

faqs.question: What is Reawakened on Roblox?
faqs.answer: Reawakened is a Solo Leveling-inspired Roblox RPG developed by Vayron Studios. Players choose a class, fight through ranked gates, upgrade traits and gear, and climb the hunter rank ladder in a dark anime-style dungeon combat game.

faqs.question: Is Reawakened free to play?
faqs.answer: Yes. Reawakened is free to play on Roblox. Cosmetic items may be available for Robux, but core gameplay including all classes, gates, and traits does not require spending.

faqs.question: How do I redeem codes in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Open the in-game menu, tap the Codes button, enter the code exactly as listed, and press Confirm. Codes are case-sensitive and may have limited uses. Check our Codes page for active codes.

faqs.question: What is the best class in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Mage has the highest gate clear speed and is the top choice for solo farming. Warrior is the safest beginner class because of high base HP. Both are strong starts in the current version.

faqs.question: How do I unlock higher rank gates in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Reach the required hunter rank by grinding XP in your current gate tier, then complete the rank-up challenge that unlocks at the level cap. Passing the challenge promotes you to the next rank and opens harder gates.

faqs.question: When are new Reawakened codes released?
faqs.answer: Codes are usually released during major updates, player milestone events, and developer announcements. Follow the official Discord at discord.gg/reawakened for instant code drop alerts.

faqs.question: Is Reawakened multiplayer?
faqs.answer: Yes. Reawakened supports group gate runs. Players can team up for B-rank and A-rank gates where group coordination is recommended. Solo play is fully supported for all rank tiers.

faqs.question: Where can I find the Reawakened update notes?
faqs.answer: Update notes are posted in the official Reawakened Discord server and through the Vayron Studios Roblox community page. Major updates are also covered in the official YouTube channel.

faqs.question: What are traits in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Traits are passive modifiers that stack with your class stats. They come in offense, defense, utility, and rare categories. You can reroll traits using gold earned from gate runs.

faqs.question: What is the current Reawakened status?
faqs.answer: Reawakened is live as a playtest on Roblox. New content, balance updates, and class additions are ongoing. Check the Discord and YouTube channel for the latest information.

reawakendBuildTips.title: Reawakened Build Tips
reawakendBuildTips.intro: Advanced build advice for optimizing your class, traits, and path combination at each stage of Reawakened progression.

faqs.question: What traits should Mage stack in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Prioritize magic damage multiplier traits first, then cooldown reduction for faster ability cycling, and finally utility traits that increase XP and gold per gate run. Avoid defensive traits unless you are struggling to survive.

faqs.question: Should I use Combat Path or Defense Path in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Combat Path is better for solo farming and gate clear speed at all rank tiers. Defense Path is superior when you want to push higher gate difficulties without dying, especially for Warrior and Tank builds.

faqs.question: How many Reawakened traits should I reroll early on?
faqs.answer: Reroll until you have at least two traits that directly match your class focus. One offense and one utility trait is a good starting combination for most DPS classes. Do not save gold for gear at the expense of trait quality.

faqs.question: When should I upgrade gear in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Upgrade gear when the current difficulty gate stops dropping upgrades. If every item from a gate run is already worse than what you have equipped, it is time to move to the next rank tier and farm there instead.

faqs.question: Is Healer worth playing solo in Reawakened?
faqs.answer: Healer is significantly slower for solo gate farming than DPS classes. It is best played in groups where your healing directly enables harder gate attempts. For solo progression, Mage or Warrior is more time-efficient.

reawakendCommunity.title: Reawakened Community
reawakendCommunity.intro: Connect with the Reawakened community through official channels. The Discord is the fastest source for new codes, game updates, and build discussions.

settings.name: Official Discord
settings.description: Join discord.gg/reawakened for code announcements, update news, class tier discussions, and direct contact with the Vayron Studios team.

settings.name: Roblox Game Page
settings.description: Play Reawakened directly through the official Roblox game page by Vayron Studios. All game sessions, updates, and patches are deployed through this page.

settings.name: YouTube Channel
settings.description: Watch official trailers, class showcases, and update videos at the Reawakened YouTube channel. New video drops often coincide with code releases and major updates.

settings.name: Vayron Studios Community
settings.description: Follow the developer group on Roblox for studio announcements, upcoming game previews, and developer posts about Reawakened and future projects.

reawakendUpdateLog.title: Reawakened Update Log
reawakendUpdateLog.intro: A running record of Reawakened updates, balance changes, and new content additions since the initial playtest launch.

entries.title: Playtest Launch
entries.description: REAWAKENED officially launched as a playtest on Roblox. First set of classes, gates, traits, and the core hunter progression system went live.

entries.title: Code System Added
entries.description: The in-game redeem code system was added, allowing players to claim XP boosts, stat buffs, and cosmetic rewards directly from the main menu.

entries.title: Class Balance Pass
entries.description: Mage and Warrior damage output adjusted. Assassin critical hit rate and Archer attack speed tuned for better class balance across gate difficulty tiers.

entries.title: New Gate Rank Added
entries.description: A higher rank gate tier was introduced with a new boss design, updated enemy wave patterns, and an expanded loot pool for the endgame hunter segment.

reawakendEarlyTips.title: Reawakened Early Tips
reawakendEarlyTips.intro: These are the highest-value actions for new Reawakened players in the first session. Following this order avoids the most common early mistakes and gives you a meaningful head start.

steps.title: Pick Warrior or Mage First
steps.description: If you have no preference, start with Warrior for the easiest survival experience or Mage for the fastest gate clear speed. Both classes scale well into mid-game and have straightforward stat priorities.

steps.title: Redeem All Active Codes Before Your First Gate
steps.description: Check the Codes page and redeem every active code before entering your first gate. XP boost codes active during your first run give you a compounding advantage through the early rank tiers.

steps.title: Clear Gates Fully Before Moving Up
steps.description: Run your current gate difficulty until your gear is completely upgraded from that tier. Jumping to harder gates with under-level gear wastes gate attempts and slows your overall progression.

steps.title: Invest in Traits Early
steps.description: Use gold to reroll traits after every few gate runs rather than saving up for gear. Two well-matched traits make every gate significantly easier, and the benefit compounds across all your future runs.

reawakendFishingGuide.title: Reawakened Fishing Guide
reawakendFishingGuide.intro: Fishing is more than a side activity in Reawakened. It powers up pets, creates early gold opportunities, and can produce gear that outperforms ordinary dungeon drops.

steps.title: Get a pole and find a fishing hole
steps.description: Buy a fishing pole from a town vendor and keep it in your inventory. In Grove, the easiest visible fishing spot is the golden sparkling hole just off the dock at the pond in the northern part of town.
highlights: A fishing pole only needs to be in your inventory to enable fishing
Fishing holes appear in town and throughout dungeon levels
Town pond is the simplest place to learn the interaction

steps.title: Cast, watch, and set the hook fast
steps.description: Left-click the fishing spot to cast. When the exclamation point appears above your character, hit Set the Hook quickly for a better chance to land the fish.
highlights: You do not need a separate fishing skill tree to start
Reaction timing matters more than combat stats
Quick hook timing improves consistency

steps.title: Know why fishing matters
steps.description: Fish can dramatically change your pet's attributes and resistances, which makes early dungeon runs easier. Fishing can also produce strong loot and gems.
highlights: Pet transformations are the biggest early power spike
Fishing is a practical gold-making route
Selling yellow drops from fishing is a reliable early economy loop

steps.title: Learn the four fish size tiers
steps.description: Fish are divided into four size tiers, and the size controls how long your pet transformation lasts.
highlights: Fingerling: 120 seconds
Small: 300 seconds
Lunker: 600 seconds
Flawless: lasts until the next transformation replaces it

steps.title: Remember the most useful transformation examples
steps.description: Different fish push pets into different forms with different stat packages and resistances.
highlights: Archerfish transforms a pet into a Dire Wolf
Bass transforms a pet into a Unicorn
Dory transforms a pet into a Salamander with 100% Fire Resist
Dogfish returns the pet to its original form

steps.title: Use fishing for progression goals too
steps.description: Fishing is tied into completion goals, not just utility. Reawakened includes the Master Angler achievement for catching every fish type.
highlights: Useful for collection-focused players
Supports pet experimentation
Adds long-tail completion value beyond raw gold

reawakendSideQuestsGuide.title: Reawakened Side Quests Guide
reawakendSideQuestsGuide.intro: Side quests are simple on the surface, but route management matters. Taking the right quests on the right floor saves time and avoids wasted exploration.

items.title: Quest slots and NPC behavior
items.content: You can hold up to 3 side quests at a time. NPCs with available side quests will not tell you what they want unless you have an open quest slot.
bullets: Free slots matter because they reveal more town quest options
Check town NPCs before committing to a long dungeon stretch

items.title: The three side quest patterns
items.content: Side quests repeatedly use three templates: recover a lost item on a level, kill a monster and its minions, or kill the monster that stole an item and recover it.
bullets: Recover stolen item from a target monster group

items.title: Take quests for the floor you are on
items.content: Accept side quests only for the floor you are currently playing. That keeps objectives aligned with your immediate dungeon path instead of scattering them across different levels.
bullets: Floor-by-floor play is cleaner than stockpiling mismatched quests
Keeps rewards flowing steadily while leveling

items.title: Do not fully clear a floor too early
items.content: Do not fully explore a floor before finishing that floor's side quests. Quest objectives can appear in unexplored sections.
bullets: Finish side quest objectives before full exploration
Prioritize quest markers and unexplored branches first
This is the main routing mistake new players make

items.title: What side quests reward
items.content: Side quests provide fame, experience, and gold. They also help pace early progression better than random wandering alone.
bullets: Gold supports shops, gambling, and utility purchases

items.title: Refresh and edge-case tricks
items.content: Available quests can be refreshed by returning to level 1 and coming back up, or by saving and exiting and then returning.
bullets: Quest list refresh: return to level 1 and back, or save and exit
Cancelable quest items can sometimes be kept
Always compare a quest reward against the item itself

reawakendWeaponsGuide.title: Reawakened Weapons Guide
reawakendWeaponsGuide.intro: Weapon choice is tied to speed, stat requirements, and the specific unique item pool. Elite and Legendary versions scale based on fame rank, making late-game weapon access a real progression goal.

items.why: Fast early sword with Attack +40 and Damage Bonus +4. Community build advice recommends sword-class weapons and dual wielding for early damage efficiency.
Grants Critical Strike +5, Shield Battle +5, Magic Drop Rate +5, and Gold Drop Rate +20% — ideal for loot-focused builds.
Bow/Crossbow Skill +5, Damage +7%, Walk Speed +10%, and Dexterity +10% make this a strong ranged unique for mobile builds.
Comes with Strength +6, Fire Resistance +40, and Knockback +2 — a strong choice for players wanting survivability alongside ranged damage.
Heavy early option with Damage Bonus Undead +10, flat Damage +7, and Magic Resistance +3% for tanky dungeon builds.
A clean example of spear scaling with Dexterity rather than raw Strength — best for builds that prioritize agility over brute force.
Hybrid staff with Attack Magic +4, Charm Magic +4, Mana Stolen +15%, and Attack +20 — covers both magic offense and mana sustain.
Elite doubles base stats, Legendary quadruples them. Legendary variants require higher Renown rank — fame directly gates late-game weapon access.

reawakendSpellsGuide.title: Reawakened Spells Guide
reawakendSpellsGuide.intro: Spells are divided into Attack, Defense, and Charm lines. Utility spells such as Town Portal, Identify, and Mental Map are just as important as raw damage for efficient play.

categories.title: Attack Spells
categories.description: Attack Magic starts with efficient early spells and scales into stronger burst tools. Slow and Fireball are core early purchases.
examples: Slow — slows target to 60% speed for 10 seconds
Fireball — projectile for 2-7 damage plus skill scaling
Frost — two ice projectiles and a 40% slow for 5 seconds
Lightning Strike — instant electric damage
Meteor Strike — area detonation in a 20-foot radius

categories.title: Defense Spells
categories.description: Defense Magic contains the sustain and speed tools most players rely on throughout the game. Haste and Dervish are standout utility options.
examples: Minor Heal — heals the caster for 20 HP plus scaling
Spectral Armor — raises defense and reduces damage taken
Haste — increases speed for 15 seconds
Battle Fog — doubles Attack Rating while lowering Defense Rating
Dervish — increases Attack Speed by 80% for 10 seconds
Reflection — reflects 20% of damage back to attackers

categories.title: Charm Spells
categories.kicker: Utility, crowd control, and map tools
categories.description: Charm Magic covers identification, map tools, and crowd control. Town Portal and Mental Map are essential for efficient dungeon routing.
examples: Identify — identifies items, scales with skill level
Charm Monster — charms monsters of level 6 and below for 20 seconds
Mental Map — reveals a dungeon level from level 2 or deeper
Town Portal — opens a portal from level 2 or deeper
Dispel Summoned Monster — removes summoned creatures

categories.title: Summon Spells
categories.description: Summons scale with Charm skill and last 1 minute plus 10 seconds per skill level. The active summon cap is 6 creatures at once.
examples: Summon Spider — summons 3 level 1 spiders
Summon Skeleton — summons 1 level 4 skeleton
Mystic Blade — summons 1 level 7 cursed sword
Summon Timberwolf — summons 2 level 7 timberwolves
Summon Wyvern — summons 1 level 28 venomous wyvern

categories.title: Hotkeys and Spell Handling
categories.description: Once a spell is learned, expand the spell list from the HUD and bind spells directly to function keys for quick access during combat.
examples: Hover a learned spell and press F1-F12 to bind it
Rotate buffs, heals, and utility without opening menus
Especially useful for Town Portal, Identify, and core combat buffs

reawakendGemsGuide.title: Reawakened Gems Guide
reawakendGemsGuide.intro: Gems drive a huge share of item power in Reawakened. This guide covers the best gem sources, socket rules that matter, and the stones worth chasing for melee, caster, and utility builds.

items.effect: Awards two random gemstones or summons a boss
items.best_for: Burst gem acquisition when you can handle the risk

items.effect: Removes gems from an item, but gems are destroyed
items.best_for: Replacing filler gems with stronger late-game sockets
Melee sustain and long dungeon chains without potions
Aggressive melee builds with passive retaliation

items.effect: +25% More Gold from Enemies, +6 Hammer Skill
items.best_for: Gold farming routes and hammer-focused characters

items.effect: +12% Finding Magic Items, +5% Reduced Item Requirements
items.best_for: Magic-find farming and easier gearing thresholds

reawakendFameGuide.title: Reawakened Fame Guide
reawakendFameGuide.intro: Fame feeds your Renown progression, and Renown directly changes what you can equip and how quickly your build grows. Characters that ignore Fame usually feel undergeared once item requirements start rising.

items.title: How You Earn Fame
items.text: Quest completions award scaling Gold, XP, and Fame. Fame also comes from killing unique and boss-type enemies, so normal floor clears and quest chains naturally build it over time.

items.title: What Renown Gives You
items.text: Every Renown increase grants four extra skill points. That makes Fame progression a direct power source, not just a gear requirement check.

items.title: Where to Buy It
items.text: Rollo can increase your Fame for gold. That lets you buy your way into the next Renown breakpoint when late-game weapons start asking for more than your current progression provides.

items.title: Why Gear Gets Locked
items.text: Higher-class items require Fame or Renown thresholds. If you see stronger gear with a requirement wall, the answer is usually more Fame, more gold for Rollo, or both.

items.title: Why Shops Improve
items.text: The better your Fame and level, the better items you find in stores and from monsters. This is one reason late-game gearing feels much smoother once Renown catches up.

items.title: How Difficulty Changes the Equation
items.stat: Adventurer → Superior, Hero → Exceptional, Legend → Flawless
items.text: Higher difficulties raise the item quality ceiling and make the Fame mechanic more important. On harder settings, Renown stops being optional very quickly.

reawakendEndgameGuide.title: Reawakened Endgame Guide
reawakendEndgameGuide.intro: The strongest late-game path is built around sockets, enchanting, gold conversion, and Renown breakpoints. Once you hit the first real spike, the rest of the run becomes a controlled upgrade loop.

items.headline: Start from a build that scales cleanly
items.target: S-tier dual-wield melee or A-tier attack magic mage
items.details: Dual-wield melee is the easiest and strongest endgame shell, while attack magic mage is the main high-damage alternative. Both scale well once buffs, enchantments, and better weapons come online.

items.headline: Keep the three core buffs running
items.details: These buffs are the backbone of the strongest physical route. Haste boosts attack and movement speed, Battle Fog raises attack rating, and Dervish adds a huge attack speed spike.

items.headline: Treat level 25+ as the first real power breakpoint
items.details: Enchantments become much more valuable once you are around level 25 and vendors begin selling higher-cost gear bases. This is where endgame prep truly starts.

items.headline: Fix sustain before chasing pure damage
items.details: Superior peridots are the early socket priority for sustain and superior jades are the follow-up for attack speed. Once sustain is solved, your clear speed rises sharply.

items.headline: Use enchanting as your gold engine
items.target: Buy expensive unenchanted bases, enchant once, resell
items.details: Blacksmith bases worth 50k+ begin appearing around level 20+ on Legend. Enchant each piece until it gains one successful enchant, then sell it back to a vendor for fast profit.

items.headline: Convert gold into Renown and weapon access
items.details: Late-game gold is not just for gear. It is also the fastest route into the Fame thresholds that let you equip stronger Legendary weapons and keep pace with item requirements.

items.headline: Take the second spike at level 50
items.details: Enchantments stop scaling at level 50. That makes level 50 the clean moment to revisit your main gear and push a second major jump in damage and survivability.

items.headline: Chase the best weapon bases by game
items.details: Base game and Undiscovered Realms favor the Flawless Legendary Greatsword. The Traitor Soul and The Cursed King shift the chase toward Flawless Legendary Elphame or Cyber swords.

items.headline: Do not waste slots on bad stats
items.details: Knockback lowers real DPS by pushing enemies out of your attack flow. Endgame gear wants sockets, damage, sustain, speed, and clean stat lines instead.

reawakendCharacterImportGuide.title: Reawakened Character Import Guide
reawakendCharacterImportGuide.intro: Reawakened supports a clean forward-import path inside the bundled series. The important rule is simple: move upward in release order and plan your inventory before each transfer.

items.label: Know the package you are planning around
items.content: The bundle contains all four games: FATE, Undiscovered Realms, The Traitor Soul, and The Cursed King. Your import route should follow that bundled release order.

items.label: Start in FATE 1 if you want one continuous hero
items.content: The recommended route is to begin with FATE and carry the same character forward through the rest of the bundle. This gives the cleanest upgrade path and avoids getting stuck behind a one-way transfer rule later.

items.label: Import only moves forward
items.content: You can move a hero from FATE 1 to FATE 2 and keep going forward through the series, but you cannot move that hero backward into an earlier game. Once you advance, treat it as a one-way ladder.

items.label: Most of your gear travels with you
items.content: When you move to the next game in the series, your character and items carry over. The exception is the move from The Traitor Soul to The Cursed King, where only your hero, pet, and what is inside the inventory bag come with you.

items.label: Pack your inventory before major transfers
items.content: Because the Traitor Soul to Cursed King move only keeps bag-carried items, this is the point where inventory discipline matters most. Put the gear you truly want on your hero, pet, or inventory before making the jump.

items.label: Do not plan around old original saves
items.content: Characters from the original pre-remaster games cannot be transferred into the remaster at launch. The import system to plan around is the forward carry path inside the remastered bundle itself.

reawakendHeirloomGuide.title: Reawakened Heirloom Guide
reawakendHeirloomGuide.intro: After beating a campaign in Reawakened, you can retire your hero and create a descendant. During retirement you choose one item to pass down as a family heirloom, and its bonuses are increased. Planning sockets, gems, and future generations before you retire makes the system far more powerful.

items.label: Retire after clearing the campaign
items.content: Retirement becomes available after beating the game. When you retire, your hero becomes an ancestor and you start the next run as a descendant instead of continuing with the same active character.

items.label: Pass down one item only
items.content: You choose a single heirloom item when retiring, so every slot choice matters. Pick a piece with bonuses you want to feel from the very start of a new run.

items.label: Use sockets before you lock the item in
items.content: Heirlooms gain stronger written bonuses, and socketed bonuses matter as part of that plan. Socket strategy is especially valuable because gem-boosted effects interact well with heirloom growth over multiple generations.

items.label: Know the core stat rule
items.content: Heirloom bonuses are increased by 25% each time the item is passed down. Even a small socketed bonus like +1 All Skills can round up to +2 after inheritance, which is why compact bonus-rich items scale so well.

items.label: Plan for multiple generations
items.content: A strong heirloom is built for repeat use, not just one descendant. Repeated inheritance compounds the item's current value, so long-term pieces with flexible stats, useful sockets, and broad build value outperform short-lived stopgaps.

items.label: Understand what happens to the old hero
items.content: Once retired, that hero is no longer your active character. In The Cursed King, the ancestor can later be hired again, but the normal expectation is that retirement is a handoff to the next generation.

reawakendAchievementsGuide.title: Reawakened Achievements Guide
reawakendAchievementsGuide.intro: Reawakened spreads its achievement list across pets, fishing, quests, combat, economy, and long-run progression. Tracking them in clusters while you play is far more efficient than chasing them at the end.

entries.name: Upgraded Weaponry
entries.requirement: Find and use a Magic Anvil

entries.name: Strategic Retreat
entries.requirement: Use a town portal with less than 10% health left

entries.name: The Undying
entries.requirement: Reach level 20 with a hardcore character

entries.name: There's no I in team
entries.requirement: Let your pet kill enemies 25 times

entries.name: Grate Protector
entries.requirement: Catch Peronto's Cheesehead of the Emerald Bay

entries.name: Gather 'round
entries.requirement: Kill 5 enemies at the same time

entries.name: Versatile Slayer
entries.requirement: Defeat 10 bosses with 4 different weapon classes

reawakendCheatsGuide.title: Reawakened Cheats and Console Commands
reawakendCheatsGuide.intro: Reawakened inherits classic cheat inputs from the original FATE games. These commands cover the standard progression shortcuts, spawn tools, and map utilities most useful for testing builds or revisiting content without a full grind.

cards.openInput: Original FATE & The Cursed King: Ctrl + Shift + ~  |  Undiscovered Realms: Ctrl + Shift + - then click the ? on the left side

commands.effect: Opens the cheat prompt in Original FATE and The Cursed King
Opens the prompt flow used by Undiscovered Realms

cards.note: In The Cursed King, the game keeps running while you type, so enter commands in a safe area.

cards.openInput: Use the matching prompt for the game you are in

commands.effect: Go up a chosen number of dungeon floors
Go down a chosen number of dungeon floors

cards.openInput: Use the matching prompt for the game you are in

commands.effect: Add fame
Add experience in supported games
Gain one level
Gain a large level jump and extra stat points in supported games

cards.note: Command names and exact rewards differ slightly between games.

cards.openInput: Use the matching prompt for the game you are in

commands.effect: Add a large amount of gold
Restore health

cards.note: Quickest utility commands for shopping, recovery, and low-friction testing.

cards.openInput: Use the matching prompt for the game you are in

commands.effect: Spawn an item near you in Original FATE
Spawn a creature near you in Original FATE
Spawn an item in The Cursed King
Summon skeletons with weapons in The Cursed King

cards.note: In Original FATE, summoning Cat or Terrier creates a pet rather than a hostile enemy.

commands.effect: Reveal the current dungeon map
Write a text description of the current map to maze.txt
Create a magic anvil
Create special interactable world objects

cards.note: Some spawned objects are practical tools, while others are mainly useful for experimentation.

cards.openInput: Undiscovered Realms: Ctrl + Shift + - then click the ?

commands.effect: Show what levels the Hero Items are on
Give that questgiver a new quest
Return to town

cards.note: These are the most utility-heavy commands unique to Undiscovered Realms.

commands.effect: Use blood instead of stars
Switch blood back to stars

commands.code: Weapon Rack / Small Chest / Large Chest
commands.effect: Spawn prop objects with mixed functionality

cards.note: Some props cannot be activated or opened normally, especially in town.

reawakendSteamDeckGuide.title: Reawakened Steam Deck and Controller Guide
reawakendSteamDeckGuide.intro: Reawakened officially supports Steam Deck and full controller play on PC. This guide combines the official support status with practical community findings on UI quirks, targeting behavior, and workarounds for the smoothest handheld session.

entries.label: Steam Deck support
entries.detail: Official FAQ confirms that Reawakened is playable on Steam Deck.

entries.label: PC controller support
entries.detail: Official FAQ confirms full controller support on PC.

entries.label: Widescreen support
entries.detail: Official FAQ confirms widescreen formats are supported.

entries.label: UI optimized for small screens
entries.detail: Many UI changes were made because the game was optimized for console controllers, Switch, and Steam Deck.

entries.label: Steam Deck layout
entries.detail: Steam Deck uses the same controller layout shown for gamepad play on PC.

entries.label: Button prompts
entries.detail: Controller prompts are shown as Xbox-style buttons.

entries.label: In-game rebinding
entries.detail: Controls cannot be remapped inside the game menus.

entries.label: Where to check inputs
entries.detail: Use the in-game controls menu first, then adjust anything extra through Steam input if needed.

sections.section: Known Quirks on Deck and Controller

entries.label: Small-text issue
entries.detail: At 1280 x 800, text can be harder to read because there is no in-game UI size option.

entries.label: Menu cursor drift
entries.detail: Steam Deck players report the cursor can overshoot menu targets when using the trackpads.

entries.label: Targeting feedback
entries.detail: Target circles do not appear on enemies when using a controller.

entries.label: Ranged and spell aiming
entries.detail: Lock-on can engage very late, especially for spell or ranged play.

entries.label: NPC interaction
entries.detail: Close-together NPCs can be fidgety to target — walk away and approach again facing the NPC you want.

entries.label: Controller remap route
entries.detail: Use the Steam Deck controller configuration menu rather than looking for an in-game remap menu.

entries.label: Build choice tip
entries.detail: Melee and general progression feel cleaner on controller than precision-heavy ranged or attack-spell setups.

entries.label: Portal positioning
entries.detail: When using a town portal, face away from staircases or traders to avoid awkward clipping near interactable NPCs.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Reawakened

questions.question: What is Reawakened on Roblox?
questions.answer: Reawakened is a Solo Leveling-inspired Roblox RPG by Vayron Studios. Fight through ranked gates, choose a class, build your traits, and climb the hunter ranks in a dark anime dungeon combat game.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Reawakened?
questions.answer: Open the in-game menu, tap the Codes button, enter the code exactly as shown, and press Confirm. Codes are case-sensitive and have limited uses.

questions.question: What is the best class in Reawakened?
questions.answer: Mage has the fastest gate clear speed for solo farming. Warrior is the easiest class for beginners because of its high base HP and straightforward playstyle.

questions.question: Is Reawakened free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Reawakened is free to play on Roblox. Some cosmetic items may be available for Robux, but all core gameplay including classes, gates, and traits is free.

questions.question: What are traits in Reawakened?
questions.answer: Traits are passive stat modifiers that stack with your class abilities. They come in offense, defense, utility, and rare categories. You can reroll traits using gold earned from gate runs.

questions.question: How do I unlock higher rank gates in Reawakened?
questions.answer: Reach the required hunter rank by farming XP in your current gate tier, then complete the rank-up challenge to promote to the next tier and unlock harder gates.
